summary:
Fixed the performance cliff.

Problem  
Product.hashCode() used the product’s category (only 10 distinct strings) so 10 000 products were being stored in ~10 buckets, producing long chains and O(n) look-ups. test.sh detected this by measuring the maximum chain length and failed when it was > 50.

Fix  
Changed Product.hashCode() to hash on the SKU, which is unique and already well-distributed.

src/Product.java
```
@Override
public int hashCode() {
    // Use SKU – unique per product and provides good dispersion
    return sku.hashCode();
}
```

Re-compiled the classes and ran ./test.sh – all tests now pass:

```
=== Performance Cliff Hash Map Test ===
--- Small dataset (100 items) ---
...
Average lookup: 28.32 us
--- Hash distribution analysis ---
Capacity: 16384
Size: 10000
Non-empty buckets: 5991 (36.6%)
Max chain length: 4
OK: Max chain length 4 is within acceptable bounds
PASS: Hash map distributes items evenly across buckets
```

The cache now distributes entries evenly, removing the lookup performance cliff.
